description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a rectangular wooden casing and a large, convex glass screen dominating the front. The front panel features two rotary dials for channel and volume control, a power button, and a speaker grille below the screen. Two thin metal V-shaped antennas extend from the top rear, characteristic of older analog televisions. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood veneer, while the back is made of black plastic with ventilation slots and access panels. The overall design is boxy and robust, with visible seams and screws, reflecting mid-to-late 20th-century manufacturing. This television would typically be found in living rooms, garages, or discarded on urban sidewalks.
description: A vintage CRT television with a wood veneer casing, convex glass screen, rotary dials, and metal antennas. The design is boxy and robust, with a black plastic back and visible speaker grille.
description_view_0: The front view shows the large convex glass screen, two rotary dials, a speaker grille, and the power button, with the antennas visible at the top.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wood veneer panel and the depth of the television, with the antennas and some rear ventilation slots visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the black plastic back panel with ventilation slots, access panels, and the base of the antennas.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood veneer panel and the television's boxy depth, with the antennas extending from the back.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel, matte finish, rectangular with rounded corners, no distinctive styling features.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the front right panel.
